items.hook: Sultan, The Sultan or Sultan of Turkey is a patience or solitaire card game that uses two packs of playing cards.
items.body: It is so named since a successful game rewards the player with a view of the Sultan surrounded by his harem of eight queens. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Opening of the Summer Olympics in Helsinki, Finland.
items.body: The 1952 Summer Olympics, officially known as the Games of the XV Olympiad and commonly known as Helsinki 1952, were an international multi-sport event held from 19 July to 3 August 1952 in Helsinki, Finland. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.opener: Here's one for the group chat —
items.hook: "Hold On Tight" is a song written and performed by English rock band Electric Light Orchestra (ELO).
items.body: The song is track 12 on the band's 1981 album Time and was released as its lead single. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: The Three Degrees is a 1973 studio album released by girl group the Three Degrees.
items.body: Since their formation in 1965 until 1969, the Three Degrees had released several hit singles on various labels, including Swan Records, Warner Bros. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Alas Chiricanas Flight 00901 is destroyed by a bomb after takeoff from Enrique Adolfo Jiménez Airport in Colón, Panama, killing 21.
items.body: Alas Chiricanas Flight 00901, registered HP-1202AC, was an Embraer EMB 110 Bandeirante aircraft flying en route from Colón city to Panama City which exploded shortly after departing Enrique Adolfo Jiménez Airport, on the night of July 19, 1994. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: World War I: Battle of Fromelles: British and Australian troops attack German trenches as part of the Battle of the Somme.
items.body: World War I, or the First World War, also known as the Great War, was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Central Powers. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Bruno Mars has an unexpected opening before Wembley party starts
items.body: After an eight-year absence from UK stages, Bruno Mars returned to Wembley Stadium with a polished, hit-filled show.

items.hook: The Tudor warship Mary Rose sinks off Portsmouth; in 1982 the wreck is salvaged in one of the most complex and expensive projects in the history of maritime archaeology.
items.body: In England and Wales, the Tudor period occurred between 1485 and 1603, including the Elizabethan era. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: American Civil War: Morgan's Raid: At Buffington Island in Ohio, Confederate General John Hunt Morgan's raid into the north is mostly thwarted when a large group of his men are captured while trying to escape across the Ohio River.
items.body: The American Civil War was a civil war in the United States between the Union and the Confederacy, which was formed in 1861 by states that had seceded from the Union to preserve slavery in the United States. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: "Old Mother Hubbard" is an English-language nursery rhyme, first given an extended printing in 1805, although the exact origin of the rhyme is disputed.
items.body: It has a Roud Folk Song Index number of 19334. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Proof of work is a form of cryptographic proof in which one party proves to others that a certain amount of a specific computational effort has been expended.
items.body: Verifiers can subsequently confirm this expenditure with minimal effort on their part. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Guinean President Alpha Condé survives an attempted assassination and coup d'état at his residence in Conakry.
